memoryArtifacts.headline: Family Pedigree Chart from Elizabeth Schellinger Smith

My grandmother had handwritten pedigree charts. This is one of her records.
KWCX-NTC
Elizabeth Schellinger Smith Anderson

zh.Great Burstead,Essex,England: 英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德
zh.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke於1603年5月1日出生在英國艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德。她於1624年7月22日與Joseph Hills Sr.在英國英格蘭艾可格亞特 布爾斯亞德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1650年3月24日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾登享年46歲。

nameMeanings.name: Clarke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Clark  .

History: This name was brought independently to New England by many bearers from the 17th century onward. Nicholas Clarke was one of the founders of Hartford, CT (coming from Cambridge, MA with Thomas Hooker) in 1635.

variants.name: Clark
meanings: English: from Middle English clerk, clark ‘clerk, cleric, writer’ (Old French clerc; see Clerc ). The original sense was ‘man in a religious order, cleric, clergyman’. As all writing and secretarial work in medieval Christian Europe was normally done by members of the clergy, the term clerk came to mean ‘scholar, secretary, recorder, or penman’ as well as ‘cleric’. As a surname, it was particularly common for one who had taken only minor holy orders. In medieval Christian Europe, clergy in minor orders were permitted to marry and so found families; thus the surname could become established.
Irish (Westmeath, Mayo): in Ireland the English surname was frequently adopted, partly by translation for Ó Cléirigh; see Cleary .
Americanized form of Dutch   De Klerk   or Flemish   De Clerck   or of variants of these names, and possibly also of French   Clerc  . Compare   Clerk   2 and   De Clark  .

nameMeanings.name: Rose
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Ostensibly from the vocabulary word denoting the flower (Latin rosa). However, the name was in use throughout the Middle Ages, long before any of the other girls' names derived from flowers, which are generally of 19th-century origin. In part it may refer to the flower as a symbol of the Virgin Mary, but it seems more likely that it also has a Germanic origin, probably as a short form of various girls' names based on hros ‘horse’ or hrōd ‘fame’. The Latinate form Rohesia is commonly found in documents of the Middle Ages. As well as being a name in its own right, it is currently used as a short form of Rosemary and, less often (because of their different pronunciation), of other names beginning Ros-, such as Rosalind and Rosamund .

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke was born on 1 May 1603, in Great Burstead, Essex, England. She married Joseph Hills Sr. on 22 July 1624, in Great Burstead, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 24 March 1650, in Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 46.

ja.lifeSummary: Rose Clarkeは1603年5月1日にイギリスエセックスグレアト ブルステアドで生まれた。1624年7月22日にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ブルステアドでJoseph Hills Sr.と結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1650年3月24日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスマルデンで46歳で死去した。

ko.Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Col: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 맬든
ko.Charlestown, Massachusetts Bay Colony, B: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 찰레스타운
ko.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke(은)는 1603년 5월 1일 영국 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1624년 7월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 버스테아드에서 Joseph Hills Sr.(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1650년 3월 24일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 맬든에서 46세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke nasceu em 1 de maio de 1603, em Great Burstead, Essex, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Joseph Hills Sr. em 22 de julho de 1624, em Great Burstead, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 5 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de março de 1650, em Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 46 anos.

ru.Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Col: малден, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Charlestown, Massachusetts Bay Colony, B: чарльстоун, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke родилась 1 мая 1603 г. в местности грит бурстид, эссекс, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Joseph Hills Sr. 22 июля 1624 г. в местности грит бурстид, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 24 марта 1650 г. в местности малден, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 46 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Rose Clarke nació el 1 de mayo de 1603, en Great Burstead, Essex, Inglaterra. Se casó con Joseph Hills Sr. el 22 de julio de 1624, en Great Burstead, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1650, en Malden, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 46 años.
